title: lithocarpus ochrocarpus
intro: Lithocarpus ochrocarpus, also known as the Island Chestnut, is a species of tree in the beech family (Fagaceae) native to the Pacific Islands. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Lithocarpus ochrocarpus include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea by infusing its leaves and bark in hot water, or as a tincture by steeping its roots in alcohol.
